must_fix: DATA HYGIENE (does not change verdict, fix before any future POSITIVE claim): 7/59 clues in data/professional_capable/region_b_data.json leak literal tokens of their own country name (Congo, East Timor->'Timor-Leste', Falkland Sound, Saint Kitts, Sao Tome, British Virgin Islands, Saint Vincent). region_b.py make_clue (lines 85-86) rejects only the EXACT normalized country/capital string, not token overlap. Add token-overlap rejection in make_clue: reject if _tokens(country) & _tokens(clue) is non-empty (and likewise vs capital).

DATA QUALITY (caps achievable ceiling for ALL methods, not a verdict artifact): ~6-9 model-generated clues are hallucinated or point at a DIFFERENT famous city (Norfolk Island clue reads 'Jamaica's capital'; Cayman/George Town clue describes 'Malaysia's northern state'; Jamaica/Kingston clue invokes 'River Thames'; Reunion/Saint-Denis 'Paris suburb'; Antigua/Saint John's 'Newfoundland'). Regenerate or hand-filter these clues before re-running, and disclose the noise in the report.

BASELINE FRAMING (optional, for honesty not correctness): fuzzy_lookup (validate_region_b.py:89-103) matches clue tokens against the CAPITAL name, but make_clue deliberately excludes the capital, so only 1/59 clues share a capital token while 7/59 share a country token. Add a fuzzy-vs-COUNTRY-name variant so the report does not understate trivial-lookup difficulty; the as-implemented fuzzy is matched against the wrong target.
synthesis.summary: REGION_B_BINDER_NEGATIVE HOLDS. I independently reproduced the load-bearing checks and found no harness bug that crippled the binder. (1) Span char-localization is correct: I rebuilt the exact seed-7 split and all 200 items and ran the low.find() logic against intended anchored slots -> 0 mis-localizations / 800 spans, so the binder's Qwen layer -1 span features are properly aligned. (2) Feature<->label alignment is preserved end-to-end via the ok-index (r_te[j]<->used_te[j], labels from used_tr), so no silent label-scramble manufactures a false negative. (3) Verdict arithmetic reproduces exactly (g_ablation True 0.50<=0.60; g_vs_embed False 0.592<0.883 -> NEGATIVE) and is robust even at EMBED_MARGIN=0: the binder loses 59.2 vs 83.3, a 24.1pp gap far beyond the margin. (4) The binder is NOT information-starved: its input phrases [countryA, countryB, clue0, clue1] are a SUPERSET of MiniLM's inputs plus Qwen-7B world knowledge, so the geographic-anchor leakage that helps embed is equally available to it. (5) The MiniLM baseline does NOT receive the capital, label, or country->capital table -- inputs are symmetric (clue<->country-name cosine), so it is a fair, legitimately strong baseline, not a leak. The two real fairness skews (own-country token leakage inflating embed; abstain class + WRONG_BIND_PENALTY handicapping the binder) both run AGAINST the binder, i.e. make the NEGATIVE verdict conservative rather than artifactual. The 97.9% in-sample vs 59.2% held-out gap is a genuine generalization limit (in-sample only proves the head can fit), and the +9.2pp binder-vs-ablation margin shows the signal is causally in Qwen's hidden states but weak. Prior d4e0b80 (report lines 40-42) is correctly overturned: it claimed a 70% 'win' comparing ONLY to exact 0% and fuzzy 46% with NO embedding baseline; adding the strongest non-neural retrieval baseline is the honest correction and the binder fails to beat it. The only items to fix are data-hygiene/quality (leaky and hallucinated clues) which must be cleaned before any FUTURE positive claim but do not change the current NEGATIVE verdict. No re-run is required to trust REGION_B_BINDER_NEGATIVE. Relevant files: e:/proiecte_active/D_Cortex/dcortex_repo/scripts/validate_region_b.py, dcortex_professional/region_b.py, dcortex_professional/qwen_runtime.py, scripts/train_role_evolution.py, data/professional_capable/region_b_data.json, docs/PROFESSIONAL_CAPABLE_REPORT.md.
